title: Ensure that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0

input_row.title: Ensure that the --insecure-port argument is set to 0
input_row.description: Do not bind to insecure port.
input_row.rationale: Setting up the apiserver to serve on an insecure port would allow unauthenticated and unencrypted access to your master node. This would allow attackers who could access this port, to easily take control of the cluster.
input_row.audit: The openshift-kube-apiserver is served over HTTPS with authentication and authorization; the secure API endpoint is bound to 0.0.0.0:6443 by default. By default the insecure-port argument is set to 0 . Note that the openshift-apiserver is not running in the host network namespace. The port is not exposed on the node, but only through the pod network. Run the following command: oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get endpoints -o jsonpath='{.items[*].subsets[*].ports[*].port}' Verify the return value is 6443 .
input_row.impact: All components that use the API must connect via the secured port, authenticate themselves, and be authorized to use the API. This includes: • kube-controller-manager • kube-proxy • kube-scheduler • kubelets
input_row.default_value: By default, the openshift-kube-server is served over HTTPS with authentication and authorization; the secure API endpoint is bound to 0.0.0.0:6443 and the insecure- port has been removed in Kubernetes 1.20+ .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:** 
1. Use the IBM Cloud Console to log in to your IBM Cloud account and navigate to your OpenShift cluster.
2. Open the terminal or command line interface and enter the following command to get the API server endpoints:
   ```bash
   oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get endpoints -o jsonpath='{.items[*].subsets[*].ports[*].port}'
   ```
3. Review the output of the command. 
4. Ensure that the only port listed is `6443`, which indicates that the insecure port is set to `0` (or effectively disabled). 
5. If other ports are listed, it may indicate that insecure access is configured.
6. Document the findings. If the output confirms the expected configuration, note it as compliant; otherwise, identify it as non-compliant.

**EXPECTED SECURE CONFIGURATION:** The output of the command should only display the port `6443`, indicating that the insecure port is in fact set to `0`. 

**HOW TO CONFIRM COMPLIANCE:** Compliance is confirmed if only one port (`6443`) is returned from the command run in step 2. If additional ports are returned, they must be evaluated as they may indicate potential insecure access points to the API server.
